+{"id":"gt-ingm.6","title":"Remove spawn - use pour/wisp only (see bd-8y9t)","description":"Remove bd mol spawn entirely from vocabulary.

Replace all references with:
- bd pour <proto> - Create persistent mol (liquid)
- bd wisp <proto> - Create ephemeral wisp (vapor)

'spawn' doesn't fit the chemistry metaphor. Two phase transitions (pour/wisp) are clearer than one command with flags.

See bd-XXX for Beads-side removal.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T12:38:16.595926-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T12:41:01.859024-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ingm.6","depends_on_id":"gt-ingm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-24T12:38:16.598002-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-isje","title":"Implement mol bond command for dynamic child molecules","description":"Add 'gt mol bond' command that creates child molecules at runtime with variable substitution.

Usage from mol-witness-patrol survey-workers step:
```bash
bd mol bond mol-polecat-arm $PATROL_WISP_ID \
  --ref arm-$polecat \
  --var polecat_name=$polecat \
  --var rig=<rig>
```

This creates child wisps like patrol-x7k.arm-ace with variables expanded.

Implementation:
1. Add 'bond' subcommand to mol command
2. Accept: proto ID, parent ID, --ref for child suffix, --var key=value pairs
3. Call InstantiateMolecule with Context map populated from --var flags
4. Return created child ID

Critical for Christmas Ornament pattern - without this, Witness cannot spawn per-polecat inspection arms.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T21:35:56.192637-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:43:44.601758-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T21:43:44.601758-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented mol bond command for dynamic child molecule creation"}
